comments.body: Does you CSV file have a blank (empty) line?
You shouldn't read CSV that way. You could use baeldung.com/opencsv
strongly recommended to check the size of the array returned by split(), mainly if reading user input or files
Or use Apache Commons CSV. Do not just split on commas to parse CSV.
also be aware that the 1-argument split() method will ignore empty trailing cells, that means, the returned array will have less elements
Unfortunately no, I've added the result of printing it. Printing starts from 1 (index 0), not from the CSV header.
try printing only zero indexes then see what is the result, if something strange i there you shall find out
so it read just first line as i can see in the question , why?
When I print indices 0 I am getting numbers so that's fine. It was only the first line because I've only had one record in CSV. I added another one but it doesn't matter here
is that the error line userData.setFirstName(tempArr[1]);, for sure there is a line in the file for which tempArrr has length one, try limiting the loop for two iterations only
Alright, problem solved. I knew it must have been a dumb error. The problem was an empty line in my CSV file after all records. Cheers!
BTW usually the header is also separated by commas (or better, the same separator as the data rows)

answers.body:
I don't know much about csv and room , but i read somewhere the header is also counted as a row

So

i think when you say line = br.readLine() it read the header of the csv file for the first iteration, and that header does not have any comma so line.split only returns ome string , and when you say temArr[1] it is wrong because there is only one string in the array and it is at zero index.

You should try printing that array then you will know weather or not i am right, and please also let me know, cuz it is just an idea, i am not sure.

And if that turns out to be the case you can you br.readline before entering the while loop and it will work an you expected

comments.body: I also recommend using the 2-parameter split() method with -1 or eventually the number of cells as 2nd argument, specially if used for reading CSV-like data and testing the number of elements (the 1-parameter one does discard empty trailing cells)

answers.body:
Poor CSV handling, not a Hibernate problem

Your issue is poor handling of CSV data import. Your input file contains a blank line at the bottom. A terminating blank line is common, and your code should allow for that.

Your issue has nothing to do with Hibernate nor Jakarta Persistence.

Tip: Use any of the multiple good CSV parsing/generating frameworks available in the Java ecosystem. Do not roll-your-own CSV processing.

Terminal line

Your error tells us the problem:

java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: Index 1 out of bounds for length 1

The array has a size of one. Your last line has a single field.

Then your code asks for the second element, with tempArr[1] meaning "get second element" per zero-based index counting. You are trying to access the second element of the array, but there is no second element. Hence the index-out-of-bounds exception.

That line with a single-field is almost certainly just an empty line meant to terminate the file.

Re-creation

We can recreate your situation.

If we split on an empty string, we get an array of one element whose first element (index 0) is an empty string.

String input = ""; // Empty line.
String[] output = input.split( "," );
IO.println( "Length (size) of array: " + output.length );  // Size of array is 1, there is a single element.
IO.println( "Count code points in element index 0: " + output[ 0 ].codePoints().count() ); // But that element is an empty string.
IO.println( output[ 1 ] ); // Throws java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: Index 1 out of bounds for length 1.
IO.println( "output = " + Arrays.toString( output ) );  // Before Java 25, swap out `IO` for `System.out`.

When run:

Length (size) of array: 1
Count code points in element index 0: 0
Exception in thread "main" java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: Index 1 out of bounds for length 1
    at Ex23@1.0-SNAPSHOT/work.basil.example.csv.ExTrySplitOnEmptyText.main(ExTrySplitOnEmptyText.java:13)

Solution

Either you need to tell the publisher of your data to stop including an extra blank line at the end, or you need to deal with that blank line.

To deal with that blank line, detect it. Check the size of array returned by split. Here is a simplistic example:

String[] tempArr = line.split(",");
if ( temper.length == 1 ) { … do last line stuff … }
else if ( temper.length == 7 ) { … do line processing stuff … }
else { … do error-handling stuff … }

Solution in modern Java

If you insist on managing the CSV yourself rather than use a CSV framework, do something like the following using modern Java facilities like NIO.2 and streams.

Specify a place for the file.

Path path = Paths.get( System.getProperty( "user.home" ) , "example.csv" );

Write your sample data to file.

String content = """
        1,Reanna,Colepio,159031625,reanna_colepio123@gmail.com,London,Glasgow
        2,Rita,Das,987443767,ritadas@outlook.com,Edinburgh,Glasgow
        
        """;
try
{
    Files.writeString( path , content ); // Writes UTF-8.
    IO.println( "INFO String written to file: " + path.toAbsolutePath() );
}
catch ( IOException e )
{
    System.err.println( "ERROR Error writing to file: " + e.getMessage() );
    e.printStackTrace();
}

Define a record class to for each entity parsed from a line. Add a static factory method to process our array of fields.

record Delivery( int id , String firstName , String LastName , String phone , String email , String pickup , String destination )
{
    static Delivery from ( final String[] fields )
    {
        return new Delivery(
                Integer.parseInt( fields[ 0 ] ) ,
                fields[ 1 ] ,
                fields[ 2 ] ,
                fields[ 3 ] ,
                fields[ 4 ] ,
                fields[ 5 ] ,
                fields[ 6 ]
        );
    }
}

Read the file as a stream of lines. For each line, pass to the static factory of Delivery record class. We get back a Delivery object, which we dump to console.

We use try-with-resources syntax to automatically close both the stream and its backing file.

try (
        Stream < String > lineStream = Files.lines( path , StandardCharsets.UTF_8 )
)
{
    // Process the stream: print each line
    System.out.println( "INFO Reading lines from file." );
    lineStream.forEach( line -> {
        String[] fields = line.split( "," );
        switch ( fields.length )
        {
            case 7 -> IO.println( Delivery.from( fields ) );
            case 1 -> IO.println( "« EMPTY »" );  // Last line. Ignore line by replacing println with pair of braces `{}`.
            default -> IO.println( "ERROR Found " + fields.length + " fields in line:  " + line );
        }
    } );
}
catch ( IOException e )
{
    System.err.println( "ERROR Error reading file: " + e.getMessage() );
    e.printStackTrace();
}

Example usage.

Path path = Paths.get( System.getProperty( "user.home" ) , "example.csv" );
ExCsvWithTerminalLine app = new ExCsvWithTerminalLine( path );
app.writeFile( path );

app.readFile( path );

When run.

INFO String written to file: /Users/basil_dot_work/example.csv
INFO Reading lines from file.
Delivery[id=1, firstName=Reanna, LastName=Colepio, phone=159031625, email=reanna_colepio123@gmail.com, pickup=London, destination=Glasgow]
Delivery[id=2, firstName=Rita, LastName=Das, phone=987443767, email=ritadas@outlook.com, pickup=Edinburgh, destination=Glasgow]
« EMPTY »

Entire code for your copy-paste convenience.

package work.basil.example.csv;

import java.io.IOException;
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
import java.nio.file.Files;
import java.nio.file.Path;
import java.nio.file.Paths;
import java.util.stream.Stream;

public class ExCsvWithTerminalLine
{
    private void writeFile ( final Path path )
    {
        String content = """
                1,Reanna,Colepio,159031625,reanna_colepio123@gmail.com,London,Glasgow
                2,Rita,Das,987443767,ritadas@outlook.com,Edinburgh,Glasgow
                
                """;
        try
        {
            Files.writeString( path , content ); // Writes UTF-8.
            IO.println( "INFO String written to file: " + path.toAbsolutePath() );
        }
        catch ( IOException e )
        {
            System.err.println( "ERROR Error writing to file: " + e.getMessage() );
            e.printStackTrace();
        }
    }

    private void readFile ( final Path path )
    {
        try (
                Stream < String > lineStream = Files.lines( path , StandardCharsets.UTF_8 )
        )
        {
            // Process the stream: print each line
            System.out.println( "INFO Reading lines from file." );
            lineStream.forEach( line -> {
                String[] fields = line.split( "," );
                switch ( fields.length )
                {
                    case 7 -> IO.println( Delivery.from( fields ) );
                    case 1 -> IO.println( "« EMPTY »" );  // Last line. Ignore line by replacing println with pair of braces `{}`.
                    default -> IO.println( "ERROR Found " + fields.length + " fields in line:  " + line );
                }
            } );
        }
        catch ( IOException e )
        {
            System.err.println( "ERROR Error reading file: " + e.getMessage() );
            e.printStackTrace();
        }
    }

record Delivery( int id , String firstName , String LastName , String phone , String email , String pickup , String destination )
{
    static Delivery from ( final String[] fields )
    {
        return new Delivery(
                Integer.parseInt( fields[ 0 ] ) ,
                fields[ 1 ] ,
                fields[ 2 ] ,
                fields[ 3 ] ,
                fields[ 4 ] ,
                fields[ 5 ] ,
                fields[ 6 ]
        );
    }
}

    static void main ( )
    {
        Path path = Paths.get( System.getProperty( "user.home" ) , "example.csv" );
        ExCsvWithTerminalLine app = new ExCsvWithTerminalLine( );
        app.writeFile( path );

        app.readFile( path );
    }
}

In real work, I would put JSpecify @NonNull annotations on those parameters. And I would perform null-checks with Objects.requireNonNull. I would do validation too, such as the fields argument having exactly 7 elements, and the strings being non-blank, on Delivery class.

title: Index 1 out of bounds for length 1 error while creating the array automatically from the CSV file
body:
The problem seems to be trivial but I still struggle on it. The error is java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: Index 1 out of bounds for length 1 from which I understand only the first index from the array can be accessed (id which is the primary key in that case). Why is that? Don't I determine the length of the array automatically based on the number of items separated by delimiter when I load the file CSV file ? What is wrong with my readFile() method then ?

UserData.java

import javax.persistence.*;

@Table
@Entity(name="users")

public class UserData {



    @Id 
    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    private Integer id;

    @Column
    private String firstName;

    @Column
    private String lastName;

    @Column
    private int telephoneNo;

    @Column
    private String email;

    @Column
    private String pickUp;

    @Column
    private String dropOff;

    public UserData(){}


    public UserData(String firstName, String lastName, int telephoneNo, String email, String pickUp, String dropOff){
        this.firstName = firstName;
        this.lastName = lastName;
        this.telephoneNo = telephoneNo;
        this.email = email;
        this.pickUp = pickUp;
        this.dropOff = dropOff;
    }



    public String getFirstName() {
        return firstName;
    }

    public void setFirstName(String firstName) {
        this.firstName = firstName;
    }

    public String getLastName() {
        return lastName;
    }

    public void setLastName(String lastName) {
        this.lastName = lastName;
    }

    public int getTelephoneNo() {
        return telephoneNo;
    }

    public void setTelephoneNo(int telephoneNo) {
        this.telephoneNo = telephoneNo;
    }

    public String getEmail() {
        return email;
    }

    public void setEmail(String email) {
        this.email = email;
    }

    public String getPickUp() {
        return pickUp;
    }

    public void setPickUp(String pickUp) {
        this.pickUp = pickUp;
    }

    public String getDropOff() {
        return dropOff;
    }

    public void setDropOff(String dropOff) {
        this.dropOff = dropOff;
    }
}

sample.csv

1,Reanna,Colepio,159031625,reanna_colepio123@gmail.com,London,Glasgow
2,Rita,Das,987443767,ritadas@outlook.com,Edinburgh,Glasgow

ManageData.java

import org.hibernate.Session;
import java.io.BufferedReader;
import java.io.FileReader;
import java.io.IOException;

import java.util.Scanner;

public class ManageData {

    public static void readFile() {
        String line = "";

        Session session = HibernateConfig.getSessionFactory().openSession();

        try (BufferedReader br = new BufferedReader(new FileReader("/Users/ggabrychowicz/IdeaProjects/Bus Managing System/src/main/java/sample.csv"))){

            while ((line = br.readLine()) != null){
                String[] tempArr = line.split(",");

                UserData userData = new UserData();


                userData.setFirstName(tempArr[1]);
                userData.setLastName(tempArr[2]);
                userData.setTelephoneNo(Integer.parseInt(tempArr[3]));
                userData.setEmail(tempArr[4]);
                userData.setPickUp(tempArr[5]);
                userData.setDropOff(tempArr[6]);

                session.beginTransaction();
                session.save(userData);
                session.getTransaction().commit();

            }
            session.close();
        }

        catch (IOException e) {
            if (session!=null){
                session.getTransaction().rollback();
            }
            e.printStackTrace();
        }
    }
}

That's what I get when I print the array:

1
Reanna
Colepio
159031625
reanna_colepio123@gmail.com
London
Glasgow
2
Rita
Das
987443767
ritadas@outlook.com
Edinburgh
Glasgow
Exception in thread "main" java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: Index 1 out of bounds for length 1
    at ManageData.readFile(ManageData.java:26)
    at Main.main(Main.java:4)
